source_text: JEKYLL, CAPTAIN HERBERT, C.M.G. (1885).—Lieutenant royal engineers, 17th April, 1866; employed under War Office and Trinity House in submarine mining and destruction of sunken ships, from 1868 to 1870, and under G.P.O. in the telegraph department, from 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; served in the campaign of 1878-74 on the Gold Coast under Sir Garnet Wolseley; private secretary to the Earl of Carnarvon, Jan., 1877, to 3rd Feb., 1878; secretary to the colonial defence commission, 1878; secretary to the royal commission on the defence of British possessions and commerce abroad, September, 1879; private secretary to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of Ireland, June, 1885, to Jan. 1886.

JELF, ARTHUR SELBORNE, C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhr.); 2nd cls. class. mods., 1897; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1899; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1901; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Selangor, Dec., 1903; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1905; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, March, 1905; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Pahang, March, 1907; ag. asst. sec. to res., Perak, Oct., 1908; asst. general adviser to Johore govt. Jan., 1911; offr. of cls. IV., 1st Jan., 1912; ohmn., san. b'd., Kinta, Perak, Apr., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., 16th Sep., 1914; lieut., M.S.V.R., 20th Jan., 1915; seconded for mil. serv. (lieut., general list), Apr., 1917; offr. of cls. III., 12th Dec., 1917; tempy. capt., general staff, S. Sttlmts. command; attchd. general staff, War Office (milit. intell.), Jan., 1918; H.M. Petroleum Executive (sec. to Lord Harcourt's comtee.), June, 1918; offr., cls. II., 1st June, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Perak, Nov., 1919; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1920; dir., Malayan polit. intelligence bureau, cls. 1b, 8th Dec., 1921; mem., Br. Malaya opium comtee., Dec., 1923; ag. contr., lab., S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1924; pres., estates health comsn., Apr., 1924 (recd. thanks of S. of S.); col sec., Jamaica, Oct., 1925; ag. gov., 6th Oct., 1925 to 27th April, 1926; lieut.-col., local forces, Oct., 1927.

JOHNSTON, ALEXANDER R.—Was in the civil service of the Mauritius from the beginning of 1828 until the end of 1833; he then proceeded with the late Lord Napier to China, being appointed his lordship's private secretary; and, in 1835, he became third commissioner, which office he held until appointed deputy superintendent of the trade of British subjects in China in May, 1837; in the latter capacity he was actively employed during the war in China (for which he received the medal), and in superintending the trade in the South during the absence of the chief superintendent in the North; he also administered the government of Hong Kong for upwards of a year, from the first acquisition of that island to its being finally taken over by the colonial department; since 1843, he filled the office of secretary and registrar to the superintendency of trade in China; and on the abolition of that office, Sept. 25, 1852, he obtained a compensation allowance.

flagged_value.position: administrator of the government of Hong Kong

source_text: JONES, S. B.—b. 1911; ed. Methodist Boys' High Sch., Freetown, Fourah Bay Coll., and Middle Temple; police mag., S. Leone, 1949; senr., 1958; puisne judge, 1960. (S.L. Govt. service.)

KEITH-ROACH, EDWARD, C.B.E. (1935), O.B.E. (1927), Commdr. of Order of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1885; ed. privately; 2nd lieut., 7th Lancs. Fuslers., Aug., 1914; capt., Nov., 1914; major, July, 1915; seconded, gen. staff, G.H.Q., E.E.F., July, 1915; Bim, bashi, Egyptian Army, May, 1916; intell. offr., Sudan govt., May, 1916; inspr., Port Sudan-Aug., 1916; Darfur Expedn. and first inspr., Umkedada, Darfur, Oct., 1916 (Sudan Med. and Bar); 2nd cls. mag., 1916; dist. comsnr., Eastern Darfur, Jan., 1917; passed Sudan govt. Arabic exam., 1917; 1st cls. mag., June, 1917; pub. cust., enemy property in Palestine, under occupied enemy territory administrn., Dec., 1919; 1st asst. sec., Palestine govt., 11th Oct., 1920; lent to C.O. by Palestine govt. and ag. prin., Feb., 1924 to Feb., 1925; asst. dist. comsnr., N. dist., 1st Oct., 1925; dep. dist. comsnr., Jerusalem divn., 12th Nov., 1926; publications, "Darfur, a Province of the Anglo-Egyptian Sudan," "The Pageant of Jerusalem," joint editor, "Handbook of Palestine, 1922"; edr., "Gen. Regs. of the Govt. of Palestine."
KENNAN, MYLES.—B. 1894; clk., Basutoland, 13; war serv., 1915-18; sub-inspr., Basutoland counted pol., 1926; clk., dist. comsnr., 1936.

KERSHAW, HENRY VALDER. — Capt., 19th Batt., London Regt., adjutant Nairobi defence force; served as lieut. in 2nd Middlesex Regt. in S. African War, 1899-1902 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps); asst. traffic supt., Jamaica govt. rlwy., Apr., 1906; travelling auditor, Lagos rlwy., Apr., 1908; ag. chief acctnt., Sierra Leone govt. rlwy., Mar., 1909; asst. acctnt., Lagos rlwy., June, 1909 to Dec., 1910; asst. chief acctnt., Uganda rlwy., 23rd Oct., 1912 to 31st Mar., 1915; deputy chief acctnt., Uganda rlwy., 1st Apr., 1915.

flagged_value.position: assistant traffic superintendent, Jamaica government rlwy.
acting chief accountant, Sierra Leone government rlwy.

source_text: KING, CHARLES KEASLEY BERRY.—Ed. Stony-hurst Coll.; matric. Lond. Univ., 1855; 1st clk. and 3rd sub-imigrn. agt., Br. Guiana, Nov., 1871; 2nd sub-imigrn. agt., June, 1872; sen. ditto, Apr., 1881; acted as imigrn. agt.-gen., 1881-2, 1888-9, 1892-3-4-7; mem. of ct. of policy on last three occasions; also mem. poor law bd., and ag. chmn., 1889 and 1894; res. 1896. In 1895, one of the comsns. to report on the best scheme for commutation by E. Indian immigrants of the right of return passages to India; and in 1896, senior mem. of special comtee. to deal with allotment of ld. to I. I. immigrants in lieu of return passage.

KING, HOWARD THOMPSON MCKENZIE.—Clerical asst., comsry. dept., B. Guiana, 1st July, 1882; ag. asst. comsry., June, 1884 and Sept., 1885; confirmed as asst. comsry., 5th July, 1887; dist. comsry., 1st Dec., 1890; comsnr. of N.W. dist., and stip. mag., 9th July, 1907.

KING, JOSEPH ARTHUR. — Copyist. registrar's office Br. Guiana, Jan., 1878; ag. 3rd asst. sworn clk., Mar., 1882; 4th asst. sworn clk., Oct., 1882; 2nd ditto, July, 1883; passed exam. for certif. as sworn clk. and notary public, June, 1885; sworn clk. and notary public, Oct., 1892; admitted solr. of sup. ct., B. Guiana, July, 1894; ag. stip. mag., N.W. dist., May, 1897 to Feb., 1898; J.P. and comsnr. of affidavits; crown solr., Mar., 1898; acted as official receiver for short period in 1906.

KING, NAPLETON WALTER. —B. 1876; cler. asst., surg.-gen's office, Br. Guiana, Apr., 1893; 6th cls. clk., off. of inspr. of prisons, 1896; 5th ditto, June, 1897; 4th cls. offr. of cust., Jan., 1906; asst. supt., alms house, June, 1912; asst. supt., prisons and asst. to inspr., Sept., 1916; ag. supt., prisons and mag., Bartica, Oct.-Dec., 1917; ag. supt., Onderneeming indus. schl. and immigrn. agt., No. 4 dist., Dec., 1918-Jan, 1919 and from Nov., 1920-Feb., 1921; dep. to D.P.W., June, 1919; ag. man. dir., col. steamer serv., Mar.-Dec., 1921; traffic supt., col. transport dept., Jan., 1922; supt., prisons, inspr. of ditto and mag., Bartica, June, 1922.

KIRSOPP, GEORGE DOUGAL.—Asst. collr. cust., E.A. Prot., Jan., 1914; collr., cust., Kenya Col., Jan., 1920; compr., cust., Zanzibar, Mar., 1923; ag. treas. for various periods, 1924-31; treas. and compr., cust., Jan., 1932; comsnr., cust., Kenya-Uganda, 1934.

KIRSOPP, GEORGE DOUGAL.—Asst. collr. cust., E.A. Prot., Jan., 1914; collr., cust., Kenya Col., Jan., 1920; compr., cust., Zanzibar, Mar., 1923; ag. treas. for various periods, 1924, 1926, 1928, 1929 and 1931.

KNIGHT, WALTER AUGUSTUS. — Copyist, G.P.O., Grenada, May, 1903; 2nd clk., G.P.O., June, 1904; clk., supt. of wks., Jan., 1906; clk., pol. mag., S. Dist., May, 1907; rev. offr. and road survr., W. Dist., Jan., 1909; addtnl. coroner, St. Mark's parish, Jan., 1913; road survr., N. Dist., Dominica, Dec., 1914; comsnr., Carriacou, Dec., 1935.

KNIGHT, WALTER AUGUSTUS. — Copyist, G.P.O., Grenada, May, 1903; 2nd clk., G.P.O., June, 1904; clk., supt. of wks., Jan., 1906; clk., pol. mag., S. Dist., May, 1907; rev. offr. and road survr., W. Dist., Jan., 1909; addtnl. coroner, St. Mark's parish, Jan., 1913; road survr., N. Dist., Dominica, Dec., 1914; comsnr., Carriacou, Dec., 1935.

LAFFER, HON. GEORGE RICHARDS.—M.P. for Alexandra, S. Australia; sometime chmn. of comtees.; comsnr. of crown lands and imigrn. and min. of repatriation, 8th Apr., 1920; comsnr. of crown lands and imigrn. and min. of irrigation and repatriation, 1922-24; speaker, H. of A. since 17th May, 1927.

flagged_value.position: commissioner of crown lands and imigrn. and minister of repatriation
commissioner of crown lands and imigrn. and minister of irrigation and repatriation

source_text: LAING, ERNEST ALEX. RONALD.—L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., Edin., L.F.P.S., Glasgow, 1895; L.M. Rot. Hosp., Dub., 1894; (Queen's Coll., Belfast, Surg. Hall, Edin., and R.C.S., Dub.). Health offr., special plague duty, govt. of Egypt; res. pupil, Jervis-st. Hosp., Dub.; res., Rot. Hosp., Dub.; med. offr., Tardis dist., Bombay, and asst. health offr. on spec. plague duty, Calcutta; asst. surgeon, Hong Kong, 1902; and med. offr. in charge of govt. asylums.
LANE, CHARLES ROBERT WILLIAM. — Impl. Br. E. Africa Co.; asst. dist. supt., Oct., 1894; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 1st July, 1895; collr. 16th Sept., 1897; sub-comsnr., 1st July, 1904.
LANG, HON. JOHN THOMAS.—M.L.A. of New South Wales; treas. in Storey and Dooley govts.; premier, N.S.W., 1925-27 and since Oct., 1930.
LAURENCE, HON. SIR PERCIVAL MAITLAND, K.C.M.G. (1911); Kt. BACH. (1908).—Puisne judge, Griqualand W., Cape Col., 1882; judge pres., 1888.

LAUDER, JOHN ILES.—2nd clk., registar. and prov.-marshal's offr., Dominica, Jan., 1901; ag. asst. mast., Dominica gram. schl., 8th May to 4th Aug., 1905; govt. offr., treasury, Sept., 1905; clk. to comsnr. and clk. of couns., Montserrat, Oct., 1906; educnl. dist. offr., Aug., 1907; deputy coroner, Dist. "I," Mar., 1911; mem. of road bd., Apr., 1911; ag. curator, botanic station, 20th Jan. to 13th Feb., 1912; J.P., Mar., 1912; ag. mag., Dist. "I," 10th to 21st Nov., 1911 and 14th to 25th May, 1912; ag. offr. in charge of prison discipline, 2nd May to 22nd June, 1912; hon. sec. and mem. of pub. lib. comtee., Apr., 1908, to June, 1912; mem. of Montserrat defence reserve, Nov., 1906, to June, 1912; asst. rec.-gen., Gambia, 23rd June, 1912; ag. rec.-gen., on various occasions, 1912-17; ag. master of govt. vessels from Oct., 1912 to Oct., 1913; provisional mem. exec. and legis. couns., on various occasions, 1912-18; mem. of navigation and pilotage bd., 25th May, 1916; ag. curator of intestate estates, June to Oct., 1914; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, on various occasions, 1916-18; ag. asst. col. sec., Feb. to June, 1918, and from Dec., 1918 to Feb., 1919; food cont., June to Dec., 1918; dep. compt. of customs, G. Coast, 28th May, 1919; ag. compt., June to Dec., 1919, and Jan. to June, 1920; contrlr. of enemy property, July, 1919 to Feb., 1920 and from Feb. to Aug., 1921; compr., cust., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1922; ag. col. treas., 4th Feb. to 29th Apr., 1925; ag. col. sec., 29th Sept. to 12th Oct., 1926; govt.'s dep., 1st to 8th Oct., 1926.

LEWIS, ARTHUR KENNEDY. —Ed. at Wesleyan high schl. and C.M.S. grammar schl., S. Leone; med. dresser attached to Anglo-French boundary comsn., Dec., 1891, to Apl., 1892 (recd. thanks of govt.); clk. to D.C.; clk. of ct. of requests; registr. of births and deaths, W. dist., 1893 to 1895; 3rd clk. registr.-gen.'s dept., 1895 to 1897; 2nd clk., atty.-gen.'s off., 1898; travelled with and clk. to atty.-gen. during trial of insurgents by Judge G. A. Bonner, 1898; transport clk., N.A. dept., 1899; ditto P. W. D., Jan., 1900, to July, 1901; offr. in charge of transport on govr.'s tours, 1899-1901; regisn. offr., G.P.O., Freetown, Aug., 1901, to Jan., 1903; asst. postmr., Gambia, Feb., 1903.

LEWIS, JOHN WILLIAMSON, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. King's schl., Canterbury; Crystal Palace schl. of practical engnrg; jr. asst. engnr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rlys., 1909; asst. engnr., 1912; grade III, 1913; senr. dist. engnr., Nov., 1923; ditto, Bahru, May, 1930; do., head office, Mar., 1931; maintenance and new wks. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., Aug., 1932; ag. ch. engnr., ways and wks., Oct., 1934.

LIGHTBOURN, James Le Cato, I.S.O. (1948).—b. 1899 ; ed. Queen's Coll., Nassau, Bah. ; apothecary, govt. hosp., 1904 ; cadet, Out Island admin., 1910 ; sec., bd. of educ., 1914 ; cl. staff, gr. II, 1926 ; gr. I, 1927 ; audr., pub. acctns., Bah., 1939 ; sec., police comsnr., 1921–22.

IGHT, JOSEPH.—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. , Nov., 1921; attd., Galle kach., Jan., 1922; o, Kegalla kach., Feb., 1923; office asst. to govt. agt., Kegalla, Aug., 1923; pol. mag., talam, Apr., 1925; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1927; o, Jaffna, Apr., 1928; asst. govt. agt., ticaloa, Jan., 1929; asst. sttlint. offr., Jan., 9; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, June, 1933; do., lutara, Apr., 1934; addnl. asst. govt. agt., tara, Nov., 1935.

LIGHTBOURN, J. L.—B. 1889; cadet, Out Islands, Bahamas, 1910; ag. 3rd and 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, 1910-12; sec., bd. of educn., Mar., 1914; sec., pol. comsn., 1921-22; ag. audr., pub. accts., May-Nov., 1924 and from Jan. to Apr., 1926; ag. comsnr., Out Islands, Nov., 1926 to May, 1927; clk., grade I., audit dept., July, 1927; ag. audr., in 1929, 1930 and 1931.
LUCAS, C. A. F.—b. 1927; ed. Barrhead H. Sch., Camphill Sec. Sch. and Royal Tech. Coll., Glasgow; exec. engnr., Tang., 1952; senr., 1962; asst. dir., W.D. & I.D., 1962–64. (Tanzania Govt. service.)

McARTHY, Dennis Douglas, M.B., Ch.B. (N.Z.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), M.D. (dist.), N.Z.—b. 1899; ed. Otago High Sch., Otago Univ., Otago Med. Sch., and L.S.H.T.M. (cert.); on mil. serv. 1939-46, col.; med. offr., Zanz., 1927; S.M.O., 1946; Uga., 1946; asst. D.M.S., 1947; malaria res. offr. (C.D.F.), Zanz., 1934-37; mem., E.A. cmd. research comtee., 1941-46; author of Malaria in Zanzibar , 1934-37, and jt. author of Dengue in the Comoro Is. , Dengue Fever in the E.A. Cmd. , 1940-45.

McCARTHY, Dennis Douglas, M.B., Ch.B. (N.Z.), D.T.M. & H. (Eng.), M.D. (dist.), N.Z.—b. 1899 ; ed. Otago High Sch., Otago Univ., Otago Med. Sch., L.S.T.M. & H. cert. ; on mil. serv. 1939-46, col. ; med. offr., Zanz., 1927 ; S.M.O., 1946 ; S.M.O., Uga., 1946 ; A.D.M.S., 1947 ; malaria res. offr. (C.D.F.), Zanz., 1934-37 ; mem., E.A. cmnd. research comtee., 1941-46 ; author of Malaria in Zanzibar , 1934-37 and jt. author of Dengue in the Comoro Is., Dengue Fever in the E.A. Cmd. , 1940-45.

MCGREGOR, ALEXANDER DUNCAN.—B. 1881-2nd grade clk., registr. of mining rights office; 1901; senr. clk., 1910; asst. registr., deeds, Bloem, fontein, O.F.S., 1928; asst. registr., deeds, Pretoria, 1931; registr., deeds, O.F.S., 1934; do., Natal, 1935; registr., mining titles and Rand townships registr., 1937; mem., townships bd., 1937; mem., deeds, rules bd., 1937.

MCELDERRY, SAMUEL BURNSIDE BOYD, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 1885; ed. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trin. Coll., Dublin; math. schl., 1st cl. hons. maths. and experimental science, 1907; B.A., 1907; cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., northern dist., New Territories, 1911; ag. dept. registr., sup. ct., 1912; ag. 1st asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1913; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., S. Dist., New Territories, 1913; offr. 3rd grade, 1915; temp. in C.O. 1915-1917, and 1919; released for mil. serv., 1918; ag. ch. asst. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., Nov., 1920 to 1921; attd. to secretariat, League of Nations, opium section, 1922; represented Br. Eastern Cols. on opium question at internat. red cross confce., Bangkok, 1922; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1924; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1925; 1st pol. mag., 1925; asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., 1926; rep. Hong Kong at C.O. Confce., May, 1927; dep. ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1929; ag. ch. sec., for various periods in 1929, 1930, 1931 and 1933; gov.'s dep. on various occasions; ch. sec., Zanzibar, Dec., 1933; ag. Br. res., various periods, 1934-39.
MACKENZIE, Alasdair Francis, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.R.C.S., A.I.C.T.A.—b. 1910; ed. Archbishop Tenison's Sch., Royal Coll. of Sci. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; agric. offr., S.L., 1935; snr., 1947.

MACLEAN, George, C.B.E., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.T.M. (Liv.).—b. 1888. ed. Glasgow Univ., Liverpool Sch. T.M., Alan Milne medal, 1924; on mil. serv. 1918–20 and 1939–42, col.; med. offr., T.T., 1921; sleep. sick offr., 1926; D.D.M.S., 1935; med. advr. and dir. med. serv. Ethiopia (on secondment), 1942–44; D.M.S., Trin., 1945; author of Relationship between economic development and Rhodesian sleeping sickness. History of an outbreak of sleeping sickness in Ufipa

MALCOLM, HARCOURT GLADSTONE, O.B.E. (1918); K.C. (1910).—Priv. sec. to Sir Henry when admnstr. of Bahamas, 1892, 1893, 1894 to Sir W. F. Haynes-Smith, gov. has, 1895; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1899; ditto, Bahamas, July, 1899; H. of A., Bahamas, 1900; deputy- 1901; re-elected, 1904 and 1911; ag. comr., Aug. to Nov., 1905; Sept., 1906 to '07; July to Nov., 1910; Apr. to Dec., say to July, and Aug. to Nov., 1913; ag. att., 1913 and 1927; speaker, H. of A., re-elected, 1913, 1919, 1927 and 1928; by S. of S. for assistance to atty.-gen. in getting bills; thanked by legis. for obtaining copies of early records; Bahamas del. Canada-W. Indies confes. at Ottawa, 1920; 1925; Bahamas rep. during visit of Prince les, and parlv. ter-centenary celebrations muda, Oct., 1920; Bahamas del. to W. confce., London, 1926; prepared 1929 edition of the laws of Bahamas. LE. STANLEY FRANCIS.—B. 1897; ed. Wellesley Schl., Somerset and Wadham Coll., Oxford; 2nd mod. language mast., Wellington, Somerset, 1919-20; war serv., France, 1919; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, ; recorder of proceedings, educn. confce., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; sec. 1st regional ml. confnce., E.A. Dependencies, 1929.

MATHEWS, HENRY ADOLPHUS. — Passed through govt. training instn., B. Guiana; 1st cls. certificated schlmstr.; head-master, grant-in-aid schl., B. Guiana, 9½ yrs.; eductnl. dist. offr., 15th Nov., 1893; acted as 2nd asst. inspr. of schools on various occasions, 1895-1903.

flagged_value.position: acted as 2nd assistant inspector of schools

source_text: MATTHEWS, LESLIE GARD.—Ed. Plymouth; Oxford Univ. O.T.C., 1915; O.A.S., France, 916 and 1917, Lanc. Fusiliers; Mesopotamia, 917-11, attd., Indian Army and Iraq Levies; Arab rebellion, Iraq, 1920 (med. and clasp); asst. coms., Somaliland. June, 1921; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1923; asst. comsnr., pol., Nigeria, 1926.

MAXWELL, ARTHUR WALTER, M.I.R.S.E.—B. 1885; ed. St. Thomas' Charterhouse, Lon.; ch. tel. mech., N. Nigeria, 1910; asst. signal and tel. engnr., F.M.S. rlys., 1913; asst. tel. engnr., 1917; dist. tel. engnr., 1919; signal and tel. engnr., Feb., 1925.
MAXWELL, JOSEPH P., M.I.C.E. — Pub. wks. dept., N. Zealand, 1874; gen. man., N. Z. rlys. 1880; rly. comsnr. under Rlys. Act, 1887, Jan., 1889.
MIDDLETON, John Reginald.—b. 1918 ; on mil. serv., 1942–46, capt. ; public wks. learner survr., Kingston, J'ca., 1934 ; asst. supt., police, 1948.
MIDDLETON, HON. WM. EDWARD. —B. 1860; ed. Toronto Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; admitted solr., 1884; called to the bar, 1815; K.C., 1908; judge of high ct., Ontario, 1910.
MIDDLETON, HON. WM. EDWARD.—B. 1860; ed. Toronto Coll. Inst. and Toronto Univ.; admitted solr., 1884; called to the bar, 1815; K.C., 1908; judge of high ct., Ontario, 1910.
MILLER, J. B. P.—b. 1903 ; ed. Oxford Univ. ; educ. offr., Ken., 1947 ; senr., 1951 ; provincial educ. offr., 1952.

MILLER, Roger Wallace Rayment.—b. 1908 ; ed. Solihull Sch. and Downing Coll., Camb., B.A., dip. agric. sci. (Cantab.) ; on mil. serv., 1940–46 ; guide demonstrator, I.C.I. agric. res. stn., 1933 ; agriculturist, I.C.I. (India), 1935 ; agric. offr., Mal., 1946 ; senr. agric. offr., Sarawak, 1946 ; dir., agric., 1948.

MILLINGTON, WM. MILNES, B.A. (Oxon).—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Nov., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Jasin, May, 1910; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, 11th Feb., 1911; prom. to cla. V., F.M.S., 3rd Mar., 1911, but continued to act in Dindings; ag. asst. P.M.G., Penang, 28th Mar., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, surg.-lieut., Antigua def. force, 1904; mag. and med. offr., Anguilla and dep. judge of sup. ct., Leeward Is., Jan., 1905 to Sept., 1906; med. offr., No. 6 dist., Nevis, 1906; med. offr., dist. "D", and mag., dist. "F", Dominica, 1907; med. offr., St. Vincent, 1909-12; temp. capt., R.A.M.C.; med. offr. in charge of troops, Cambridge, 1916-19; founder and hon. sec., W. Indian Club, 1897-8; founder and hon. sec., West India Red Cross Socy., 1921-1924; dir., med. and health dept., Bermuda, Aug., 1924.

MONTAGNAC, LOUIS IVAN DE.—B. 1880; called to bar, Middle Temple, 26th Jan., 1911; clk. of cts., parish of St. Andrew, Jamaica, 1st May, 1921; ag. res. mag., St. Thomas, 1st Aug., 1922; ag. registr., sup. ct., 25th Oct., 1924; registr., sup. ct., 29th July, 1926.

MOORE, W.—Minister of lands and works, Tasmania, 4th Aug. 1878, to 20th July, 1876, and from 9th to 13th Aug. 1877; colonial secretary, 13th Aug. 1877, to 20th Dec 1878, and again Oct., 1879, to 1881; now president of leg. coun.

MORRIS, ALFRED, A.C.P., M.R.San.Inst.—1874; ed. St. Paul's, Cheltenham; head master, Saiyingpun, Hongkong, 1905-1926; normal school, Queen's Coll., 1914; lecturer, T.I. Teacher 1914-19; English tutor, H. K. Univ., 1915; master of method, H.K. Univ., 1920; ag. supt., St. John Amb. brigade, 1926; head master, King's Coll., 1927; sec., hosp. comforts, 1927; J.P., 1928; ag. dir., tech. inst., 1928; Dec., 1928.

MORRIS, HON. E. P., K.C.—Mem. Newfoundland assem., 1885-1900; ag. atty.-gen. for Newfnd., 1890-91-95; delegate to Ottawa on confederation question, 1894; delegate to London, 1899, with Rt. Hon. Sir W. V. Whiteway, on matters then before the col. office; leader independent party, Newfnd. assem., 1898-1900; mem. Newfnd. cabinet, 1889-1897-1900; master in chancery, and re-elected for St. John's West, 1900; editor Newfnd. Law Reports Revision, 1820-1900.

MORRISON, C. J.—b. 1919; ed. St. Simon's Coll., J'ca Sch. of Agric. and I.C.T.A.; agric. instr., J'ca, 1943; supvr., agric. instrs., 1947; soil consvn. offr., dept. of agric., 1951; secon., agric. offr., to Yallahs Valley land authy., 1951–53; senr. agric. offr., 1953; offr. i/c experiments and stns.; secon., Christiana land authy., 1954. (J'ca Govt. service.) ☑ MORRISON, D. B.—b. 1918; ed. Broughty Ferry Grove Academy; mil. serv., 1939–46, sgt.; Br. P.O., 1935–49; tech. gr. I, E.A.P. & T. admin., 1949; asst. inspr., traffic, 1952; asst. contrl., telecoms., 1955; senr. asst. contrl., 1959.

MORRISON, C. G. M.—b. 1917; ed. Winchester and Trinity Coll., Camb.; interned, 1941–45; cadet offr., cl. II, H.K., 1939; asst. to supt., imports and exports, 1939; attd. H.K. civ. affrs. London office, 1946; attd. sect. H.Q. civ. affrs. unit, H.K. admin., 1946; asst. P.M.G., 1947; cadet offr., cl. I, 1956; staff gr., 1957; chmn., urban coun. and dir., urban servs., 1958; chmn., housing authy., 1958; redesig. admin. offr., staff gr. A, 1959–63.

MURPHY, M. H.—b. 1911; ed. Ecole St. Jean Baptiste, Papeete, Tahiti; St. Joseph's Coll., H.K., and Lond. Radio Coll.; wireless offr., E.A.P. & T. admin., 1937; A.T.C.O., 1947; telecomm. offr., 1955; sigs. offr., 1961; senr., 1962.

MURPHY, WILLIAM HENRY WYNDHAM.—B. 1874; ed. Brighton Coll., St. Edmunds, Ware. Army tutors, Versailles; served S. African War, 1899-1902; commanded M. I. Coy., 1st Leinster Regt., Queen's and King's S. A. Medals, 8 clasps, ment. in desps.; transfd., S. A. constab.; asst. dist. comdt. and asst. res. mag., 1903; transfd., Bech. Prot. pol., 1908; rea. J.P.; Reg. Army R. of O.; served European War, France, Flanders and Rhine Army of Occupation; lieut-col., commanded a batt., 1916-19; ag. brigadier, 1916; D.S.O., 1917; ment. in desps.; transfd., Tanganyika Territory as dist. supt. pol., 1920; transfd. Zanzibar, commdt. of pol. and gov. of prisons, 1924; King's pol. med., 1928.

NICOLLS, EDWARD HUGH DYNELEY, C.M.G. (1927), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1871; A.M.I.C.E., 2nd cls. hons. certif. and bracketed first in All England in geom. and perspective drawing, Camb. loc. exams., 1886; supt. of pub. wks., Cyprus, May, 1898; 1st div. engr., Dec., 1898; ag. dir. of pub. wks., Mar., 1903; dir. of pub. wks., Jan., 1904; O/c., A.S.C., Cyprus, in addition to own duties, June, 1915, to Nov., 1916; O/c., R.E., from commencement of the war to date of leaving Cyprus on apptmt. as dir. of pub. wks., Gold Coast, 22nd Nov., 1918; granted local and tempy. rank of capt. from 1st July, 1916, to date of leaving Cyprus; ment. in desps.

NIHILL, JOHN HARRY BARCLAY, K.C., M.A., M.C.—B. 1892; ed. Felsted and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. hist. tripos, pts. I and II; pres., Union Socy., 1914; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; 2nd lieut., Munster Fusiliers, 1914-18; investigating offr., Min. of Lab., joint industrial couns. divn., 1919; priv. sec. to Sir Wm. Edge, junr. Lord of Treas., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1921; ag. asst. crown solr., 1924; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1926; ag. asst. atty.-gen., 1926; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; legal sec. to high comsnr., Iraq, 1927-31; ag. Br. cons., Baghdad in addn., 1928 and 1931; mem., Br. deleg. to coun. of L. of N., 1931; legal advr., Embassy, Baghdad, 1932-33; solr.-gen., Uganda, 1934; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1934; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1934 to Feb., 1935; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1936; puisne just., Ceylon, Nov., 1938.

NIHILL, Sir John Harry Barclay, Kt. Bach. (1948), K.C., M.A., M.C.—b. 1892; ed. Felsted and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge. 2nd cl. hist. tripos, pts. I and II, pres. Union socy., 1914, barrister-at-law, Inner Temple: on mil. serv. 1914–18, 2nd lieut.; cadet, H.K., July, 1921; ag. asst. crown solr., 1924; pol. mag., Kowloon, 1926; ag. asst. atty.-gen., 1926; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., 1927; legal sec. to high comsnr., Iraq. 1927–31; ag. Br. cons., Baghdad, in addn. 1928 and 1931; mem. of Br. delegn. to coun. of L. of N., 1931; legal advsr., Embassy, Baghdad, 1932–33; solr.-gen., Uga., 1934; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1934; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1934–Feb., 1935; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1936; puisne just., Ceylon. Nov., 1938; legal sec., 1942; ch. just., Ken., 1946.

NOOTT, P. GORDON. — B. 1880; entered army (Royal Welsh Fus.), 1898; served in Boer war, 1899-00 (Queen's medal with clasps); Tugela heights, relief of Ladysmith, Cape Colony, Orange Free State and Transvaal; seconded for serv., N. Nigeria regt., Dec., 1904; served with Dakka Kerri expedit., N. Nigeria, 1908; ret. on pension, Oct., 1913; ch. warder, Belize prison, B. Honduras, May, 1914; comsn. 2nd lieut., B. Honduras Terr. Force, 1914; lieut., 1916; served on Cayo expedit., B. Honduras, 1916; instructor to 2nd B. Honduras contingent, 1916; capt., 1918; supt. of prisons, Grenada, Jan., 1920.

NORTHEY, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR EDWARD, G.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B.—B. 1868; served with Hazara expedn., 1891; Miranzai expedn., 1891; Isazai expedn., 1892; in S. African war, 1899-02; and in the war of 1914-18; extra A.D.C. to H.M. the King, 1915; brig.-gen. (temp.) commdg. Nyasaland Rhodesian field force, 1915-18; lieut.-col. (brevet col., 1915); King's R.R.C., 1911; gov. E. Africa Prot. and high comsr., Zanzibar Prot., July, 1918; assumed govt., Jan., 1919; ret., Aug., 1922.

NORTHEY, Major-General Sir Edward, G.C.M.G. (1922), K.C.M.G. (1918), C.B.—b. 1868; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; served with Hazara expedn., 1891; Miranzai expedn., 1891; Isazai expedn., 1892, in S.A.; war, 1899–02; and in the war of 1914–18; extra A.D.C. to H.M. the King, 1915; brig.-gen. (temp.) commdg. Nyasa. Rhod. field force, 1915–18; lieut.-col. (brevet col., 1915); King's R.R.C., 1911; gov. E.A.P. and high comsnr., Zanz., July, 1918; assumed govt., Jan., 1919; ret., Aug., 1922; G.O.C. 43rd (The Wessex) Divn. T.A. and S. Western Area, 1924–26.

OLIVIER, S. J.—b. 1905; ed. Sherborne Sch.; mil. serv., 1940-41; O.E.T.A., Ethiopia, 1941-43; sec. to govt., Br. Som. mil. admin., 1943-47; cadet, G.C., 1929; admin. offr., 1932; Nyasa., 1934; dep. prov. comsnr., 1949; secon., fedl. high comsnr's off., London; ret., 1959.
OWEN, A. E.—b. 1920; ed. Colwyn Bay Modern Secondary Sch.; mil. serv., 1940–46; Br. P.O., 1934–49; techn. gr. I, E.A.P. & T. admin., 1949; asst. engr., gr. I, 1955; senr. asst. engr., 1960–62.

PERKINS, HUGH WHARTON, C.B.E. — B. 1886; ed. Berkhamsted Sch.; on active serv., Nov., 1914; D.A.R.T., June, 1915; temp. major, July, 1916; temp. lieut.-col. and asst. dir., rly. transport, Nov., 1917; C.B.E. (mil.), June, 1918; demob., Nov., 1919; ag. traf. man., F.M.S. rlys., Feb., 1925; traf. man., F.M.S. rlys., Oct., 1925.

PUDSEY, FAWCETT.—B. 1882; Assoc. M.I.M.E.; Assoc. M.I.C.E.; ed. Hyner's Coll., Hull, and Repton Schl.; apprentice with Messrs. Rose, Downs and Thompson, Ltd., Hull, 1900-1905; engineering asst., dock engnr.'s staff, N.E. Rly., 1905-1911; engaged on survey work, Catalonia, Spain, for the Pearson Engineering Corporation, New York, 1911; in charge of opening of the Hessle quarry and crushing plant of the British Portland Cement Manufacturers, Ltd., 1912-1914; asst. D.P.W., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. D.P.W., Feb., 1916; D.P.W., Palestine, Dec., 1927.

PRINCE, PROF. EDWARD ERNEST, F.R.S.C., &c. — B. 1858; ed. Modern Schl., Leeds, and St. Andrews, Camb., and Edin. Univs.; apptd. demonstr., zool., Edin. Univ., 1885; naturalist, Scotch fishery bd. laboratory, St. Ands.; prof. of zool. and comp. anat., Royal Infirmary Med. Coll., Glasgow, 1890; fishery expert, Irish Deep Sea Survey, 1893; vice-pres. Internat. Fisheries Congress, Washington, Paris, and St. Petersburg, 1903-06-09; vice-pres., biol. sect., Brit. Assoc., 1909; ditto, Royal Soc. of Canada, 1908; mem. of internat. relations coun., American fish. soc., 1910; Canad. mem. of internat. fish. congress, Rome, 1911; life mem. of British Science Guild; mem. of internat. fisheries comsn.; repres. Canada, under Fisheries Treaty, 1908; chmn. of many fishery comsans: lobster, 1898; Pacific salmon, 1907; Bay of Fundy Shad, 1908; Manitoba fisheries, 1909; Alberta and Sask. fisheries, 1910-1911; chmn. of biol. bd. of Canada; comsnr. of fisheries for Canada, apptd. 1892.

RAINSFORD, CAPT. RICHARD FREDERICK. — B. 1888; Natal pol., 1904-07, served Zulu Rebellion, 1906, (med.); B.S.A.P., 1907-08; inspr. E.A. Pol., 1910; asst. supt., 1914; commanded No. 1 Column, Turkhana Punitive Expedn., 1915 (desps., A.G.S. med.); capt., 5th K.A.R., 1916; adjt. and staff offr. to O.C. troops, Jubaland, 1917-19, (desps.); "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory meds.; supdt., Kenya pol., 1923.

REAKES, CHARLES JOHN, M.R.C.V.S., Eng., Doctor of Vet. Science, Univ. of Melbourne. — B. 1865; apptd. vet. to New Zealand govt., 1897; ch. vet., 1908; lieut.-col., N.Z. vet. corps, 1908; colonel, 1915; dir. of vet. services and remounts, N.Z. defence dept., 1908; dir., live stock div., agric. dept., 1909; dir.-gen. of agric., 1918.

REED, HON. SIR JOHN RANKEN, Kt. Bach. (1936), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1864; ed. Gram. Schl., Auckland, N.Z., Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1921-39; pres., prisons bd., 1928; judge advoc.-gen., N.Z. Defence Forces, with rank of colonel, 1911-33.
RICHARDS, HON. FREDERICK WILLIAM, LL.D.—Crown solr., S. Australia, 1916; formerly parliamentary draftsman; ag. judge, sup. ct., 1925; judge, ditto, 1927.
RICHARDSON, R. K.—b. 1918; ed. Pamphylian High Sch. and St. Mary's Coll., Trin., St. Mungo's Med. Sch., Glasgow; M.O., gr. C, Trin., 1946; gr. B, 1950; gr. A (T.B. div.), 1952. (T'dad Govt. service.)

ROBERTS, J. D.—b. 1923; ed. Canton High Sch., Cardiff; mil. serv. 1941–46; Br. P.O. 1937–1952; E.A.P. & T. admin., postmaster gr. II, 1952; sales and serv. supt., 1956; chief telecoms. supt., 1960; tele. sales mangr., 1961–62.
ROBERTS, CAPT. WILLIAM HOWARD.—B. 1887; asst. engnr., F.M.S., June, 1920; exec. engnr., June, 1929; do., P.W., Dec., 1933; do., S'pre, Oct., 1934; ag. snr. exec. engnr., Feb., 1936.

ROBERTS, GEORGE FURSE.—B. 1881; ed. Univ. Coll., London; telegraphist, G.P.O., Sept., 1897; 2nd div. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Apr., 1900; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1905; off. asst. to govt. agt., Uva Prov., Apr., 1907; pol. mag., Panadure, Jan., 1909; Jaffna, Dec., 1909; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, and comsnr. of requests and pol. mag., Nuwara Eliya, May, 1912; pol. mag., Galle, Mar., 1915; pol. mag., Colombo, Mar., 1916; tea comsnr., Nov., 1918; contr., estate supplies, in additn. to his own duties, May, 1919; comsnr., requests, Colombo. Nov., 1921; rubber contr., Oct., 1922; dist. judge, Kurunegala. Nov., 1925; ditto, Negombo, Nov., 1926; regisr.-gen. and regisr., motor cars, Nov., 1928; addnl. dist. judge, Kandy, June, 1931; comsnr., requests Kandy in addn., Oct., 1931; dist. judge, Galle, Jan., 1935.

ROCHE, CAPTAIN W. P.—Late 3rd (Prince of Wales') Dragoon Guards educated at the Royal Military College, Sandhurst; served in India, and throughout the Abyssinian campaign (medal). First writer and superintendent of police, Gambia, May, 1879; clerk of the legislative council; acting collector and treasurer, July, 1880, till Jan., 1881, and again, Jan. till May, 1881; government secretary, July, 1883; superintendent of police, Grenada, 1886.

flagged_value.position: first writer and superintendent of police

source_text: ROCHE, CAPTAIN W. P.—Late 3rd (Prince of Wales') Dragoon Guards; educated at the Royal Military College, Sandhurst; served twice in India, and throughout the Abyssinian campaign under Lord Napier of Magdala (medal). Appointed first writer and superintendent of police, the Gambia, May, 1879; clerk of the legislative council; is a justice of the peace; acting collector and treasurer from 1st July, 1880, till 14th Jan., 1881, and again from 22nd Jan. till 7th May, 1881; government secretary, July, 1883.

flagged_value.position: first writer and superintendent of police

source_text: ROGERS, W. B., O.B.E. (1945), E.R.D.—b. 1914; ed. Charterhouse; mil. serv., 1939-46, col.; cadet, Tang., 1950; estab. offr., 1950; prin. asst. sec., 1955; admin. offr., cl. IIA, 1957; cl. I, 1959; trade rep. for Tang. in Fed. of Rhod. & Nyasa., 1959; perm. sec. (ext. affairs and def.), 1961-62; devel. comsnr., Fiji, 1963; Fiji gov't's rep., Australia, 1964; hd. of security secretariat, Aden, 1965.

ROTH, WALTER E.—B.A. (Oxon), late Demy of Magd. Coll.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P. (Lond.); hon. mem. anthrop. societies of Berlin and Florence, and of anthrop. inst., Lond.; late ch. protector of aborigines, Queensland; and Royal comsnr. to inquire into condition of natives of W. Aust.; stip. mag., dep. prot. of Indians, and govt. med. offr., Pomeroon River, B. Guiana. 8th Dec., 1906; stip. mag., Demerara River, 1915; author of "Elements of School Hygiene," 1886, "Theatre Hygiene," 1888, "Ethnological Studies among the N.W. Central Aborigines, Queensland," 1897, "N. Queensland Ethnography Bulletins," "Superstitions, Magic and Medicine," 1903; "Burial Ceremonies and Disposal of the Dead," 1907, "Animism and Folk-lore of the Guiana Indians," 1915, "Arts, Crafts and Customs of the Guiana Indians," 1923, translator of Richard Schomburgk's "Travels in Br. Guiana," 1923.

ROTH, WALTER E.—B.A. (Oxon), late Demy of Magd. Coll.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P. (Lond.); hon. mem. anthrop. societies of Berlin and Florence, and of anthrop. inst., Lond.; late ch. protector of aborigines, Queensland; and Royal comsnr. to inquire into condition of natives of W. Aust.; stip. mag., dep. prot. of Indians, and govt. med. offr., Pomeroon River, B. Guiana, 8th Dec., 1906; stip. mag., Demerara River, 1915; author of "Elements of School Hygiene," 1886, "Theatre Hygiene," 1888, "Ethnological Studies among the N.W. Central Aborigines, Queensland," 1897, "N. Queensland Ethnography Bulletins," "Superstitions, Magic and Medicine," 1903; "Burial Ceremonies and Disposal of the Dead," 1907, "Animism and Folk-lore of the Guiana Indians," 1915, "Arts, Crafts and Customs of the Guiana Indians," 1923, translator of Richard Schomburgk's "Travels in Br. Guiana," 1923.

ROWELL, Thomas Richmond, C.B.E. (1950), B.Sc. (hons.), dip. educ.—b. 1896; ed. High Sch., I.O.M., Chester Coll., and Univ. of Liverpool; on mil. serv., 1915–18 and 1945–46, col.; mstr., educ. dept., H.K., 1923; prin., 1939; asst. educ. advsr., C.O., 1942; ag. educ. advsr., C.O., 1945; H.K. planning unit, 1945; dir., educ. H.K., 1946; mem., advsy. comtee. on educ. in the colonies, 1942; sec., W.I. higher educ. comtee., 1944; mem., comtee. on mass educ., etc.

SALIER, LIEUT.-COL. EDWARD LUCAS.—B. 1882; ed. Hutchins Schl., Hobart, Tasmania; 2nd lieut., 4th K.S.L.I., 1903; Northumberland Fusiliers, 1906; India, N.W. Frontier, 1908; seconded S. Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F., 1910; asst. regtl. adjt., S.N. Regt., 1910; regtl. adj., S.N. Regt., 1911-13; adjt., 3rd S.N. Regt., 1914; S.O., Fly and Cross River Columns, W.A.F.F., Cameroons, 1914; adjt., 2nd Northumberland Fusiliers, France, Belgium, Salonika, 1915; severely wounded, 1915; brig. major, 12th Australian Infy. Brig., France, Belgium, 1916-17; brevet major, June, 1917; G.S.O. II, Tank Corps, 1917; in commd., 1st Northumberland Fusiliers, 1918; G.S.O. I, Tank Corps, 1918; temp. lieut.-col., 1918; six times ment. in desps.; M.C., Legion of Honour, brevet major; in commd., 4th Nigeria Regt., W.A.F.F., 1920-21; Reg. Army, R. of O., Lieut.-Col., May, 1921; resigned, 31st Mar., 1925; contr., lab., Ascension Island (St. Helena Colony), Aug., 1925; pol. mag., offr. in ch., secretariat, ag. treas. and collr., cust. and clerk to exec. coun., St. Helena, May, 1928; govt. sec., Jan., 1929.

SANSOM, CHARLES HENRY, C.M.G. (1938)—B. 1886; ed. Plymouth and Munnamead Coll.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1923; pol. prob., 1905; regisar. of criminals, F.M.S. and S.S., 1910; asst. comsnr. of pol., Ipoh, 1911; adjt., F.M.S. pol., 1913; comsnr. of pol., Johore, 1921; supt., pol., S'pore but cont. former appt., Nov., 1924; ch. pol. offr., S'pore, Dec., 1929; offg. ch. pol. offr., Perak, Nov., 1930; comsnr., pol., F.M.S., Oct., 1931.

SCOTT, Sir Henry Harold, K.C.M.G. (1941), C.M.G. (1936), M.D., F.R.C.P. (Lond.) ; D.P.H. ; D.T.M. & H. (Camb.) ; F.R.S.E., F.Z.S.—b. 1874 ; ed. Mercers, City of Lond., Univ. Coll., St. Barts. and St. Thos. Hosp., and Copenhagen ; res. M.O., Teignmouth, Dawlish and Newton Abbot Inf. ; hse. physician, St. Thos. ; R.A.M.C. capt., S.A. war ; European war ; pathol., Cambridge Hosp., Aldershot, i/c mobile laboratory No. 18 ; Milner Res. Fellow in comp. path., L.S.H. and T.M. ; pathol., Zoological Soc. of Lond. ; gov. bacteriologist, J'ca., 1910 ; H.K., 1919 ; lect., trop. dis., Westminster Hosp. med sch. ; dir., bureau of hyg. and trop. diseases, 1935–42 ; examr. in trop. med. and surg., conjt. bd. of R.C.P. and R.C.S. ; med. sec., col. med. res. comtee. ; examr. for dip. in T.M. and T.H. and in parasit. for D.P.H. and the B. Vet. Sc., Liverpool Univ. ; FitzPatrick lect., R.C.P. ; author of A History of Tropical Medicine, Some Notable Epidemics, Post-graduate Clinical Studies, Health Problems of the Empire (with Sir Andrew Balfour), Tuberculosis in Man and Lower Animals and British Red Cross Manual of Tropical Hygiene (with Maj.-Gen. D. T. Richardson), 1946.
